data.title: Kelfred Expects Swing to Loss in 2024
data.description:
Kelfred Holdings expects a net loss of HK$21.6 million for 2024, against a net profit of HK$1.4 million from 2023, a Monday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The eyewear products company attributed the expected swing to a reduction in gross profit margins due to a decrease in the average selling price of eyewear products, a decrease in exchange gains, and a lack of demolition compensation compared to 2023.

data.title: China's Fiscal Support Package to Amplify Rising Debt, Fitch Says
data.description:
China's planned fiscal stimulus for 2025 will anchor economic growth but also result in a notable budget deficit that will add to rising government debt, Fitch Ratings said in a Friday release.

Escalating public debt was a main driver for Fitch's April 2024 revision of China's A+ sovereign rating outlook to negative.

Fitch projects China's budgeted fiscal deficit rising to 8.8% of GDP in 2025, higher than the 6.5% recorded last year.

The substantial deficit was due to a revenue drop that included a structural decrease in property-related revenue.

The rating agency sees continued growth in general government debt, potentially exceeding 60% of GDP in 2025.

Fitch expects further monetary easing and potential renminbi depreciation to aid in countering the effects of US tariffs.

Despite an ambitious growth goal of 5%, Fitch estimates China's real GDP growth to reach 4.3% in 2025, driven by local demand constraints, property sector challenges, and external pressures.

data.title: Concentra Acquires Assets of Physicians Health Center, OM Management
data.description:
Concentra Group said Monday it has acquired the assets of Physicians Health Center and OM Management.

Financial terms weren't disclosed.

The occupational health services provider will offer work injury care, physical therapy, drug testing, DOT physical exams, pre-placement exams, and other employer-related health services from its new physicians health center locations in Miami, it said, adding that the OM Management corporate office will become a satellite office for the company's CMCA group.

data.title: Sernova Biotherapeutics Taps Pericles Calias as Chief Development Officer, R&D Head
data.description:
Sernova Biotherapeutics said Monday that it appointed Pericles Calias as chief development officer and head of research and development.

The regenerative medicine company said Calias brings over two decades of global operational experience in drug product development.

"His deep expertise in leading all aspects of drug product and cell line development is invaluable to Sernova and will add momentum to advancing our Cell Pouch Bio-hybrid Organ as a much-needed functional cure for T1D in collaboration with Evotec," CEO Jonathan Rigby said.

Prior to joining Sernova, Calias held leadership roles at life sciences companies including Revolo Biotherapeutics, Cerecor, Sucampo Pharmaceuticals and Shire HGT, according to Sernova.

Sernova's shares closed 2.6% lower on Friday to $0.19 on the TSX.

data.title: S&P 500 index: Downside still beckons
data.description:
Key points:
  • U.S. equity index futures red; Nasdaq 100 down >1.5%
  • Euro STOXX 600 index off 0.8%
  • Dollar, bitcoin rise slightly; crude gains; gold slips
  • U.S. 10-Year Treasury yield falls to ~4.25%

S&P 500 INDEX: DOWNSIDE STILL BECKONS

The S&P 500 index ended Friday at 5,770.20, putting it down 6.09% from its February 19 record closing high. However, with e-mini S&P 500 futures suggesting a loss of about 80 points, or more than 1.3%, at Monday's open, the benchmark index appears poised for further downside pressure.

With this, the index looks like it will make another attempt to take out its rising 200-day moving average (DMA), which should be around 5,735:

Since reclaiming this closely followed long-term moving average on November 2, 2023, the SPX has not registered a daily close back below.

Therefore, bulls will be watching to see if the index can hold support in the 5,666.29-5,633.35 area and mount another recovery. This zone includes Friday's intraday low (5,666.29), the 23.6% Fibonacci retracement of the October 2023-February 2025 advance (5,665.13), the late-August high (5,651.62), the rising 233-day moving average, a Fibonacci-based long-term moving average (around 5,650 on Monday), and the 50% retracement of the August 2024-Febraury 2025 advance (5,633.35).

At the 50% retracement level, the SPX would be down 8.3% from its record closing high.

In the event of a deeper drop, the 61.8% retracement of the August-February advance is at 5,512.02. A decline to this level would put the S&P 500 down 10.3% from its record closing high.

Of note, in the July 2023-October 2023 selloff, which has been the most severe SPX decline since the 2022 bear market concluded, the index fell as much as 10.1% on a closing basis.

On a reversal back above the 200-DMA, initial resistance will be in the 5,773-5,783 area.

(Terence Gabriel)

*****

data.title: Sector Update: Consumer Stocks Flat to Lower Premarket Monday
data.description:
Consumer stocks were flat to lower premarket Monday with the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund down 0.3% and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund recently inactive.

Star Entertainment said it is assessing a recapitalization proposal from Bally's worth AU$250 million ($158.1 million), which could give the US-based company a controlling stake. Bally's shares rose past 9% pre-bell.

Hims & Hers Health's Apostrophe business said it's being discontinued, with all existing subscriptions to be canceled as of March 7. Shares of Hims & Hers Health were down over 3% premarket.

Wayfair shares declined by over 3% after the company said its Wayfair LLC unit plans to offer $700 million in senior secured notes due 2030 in a private offering.

data.title: Market Chatter: Namyang Dairy Returns to Profit, Targets KRW1 Trillion in Sales in 2025
data.description:
Namyang Dairy Products ended a six-year losing streak in 2024 and aims to surpass 1 trillion won in annual sales in 2025, a level that the company last touched in 2019, Pulse News reported Friday.

The company rebounded from the 2021 Bulgaris incident and management disputes by expanding product sales, exports, and business-to-business operations, to post 952.7 billion won in revenue and a net profit of 73.24 million won in 2024, the report said.

Analysts predict continued growth in 2025, with the company focusing on revamping its fermented dairy products to meet changing consumer preferences, it said.

Shares of Namyang Dairy Products fell nearly 2% at market close on Monday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Spin Master Renews Global Master Toy Partner Agreement with Universal Products & Experiences
data.description:
Spin Master on Monday renewed its global master toy licensee agreement with Universal Products & Experiences for DreamWorks Animation's popular preschool brand Gabby's Dollhouse.

The company is preparing a new toy line which will launch in the fall, inspired by DreamWorks Animation's upcoming feature film, Gabby's Dollhouse: The Movie.

Shares of the company closed up $0.42 to $26.61 on Friday on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

data.title: Qunabox Group Swings to 2024 Loss
data.description:
Qunabox Group incurred an attributable loss of 1.67 billion yuan for the year 2024, compared with an attributable profit of 130.9 million yuan in the year-ago period, a Monday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

Loss per share was 8.4 yuan, whereas the company had made earnings per share of 0.56 yuan in 2023.

The marketing service company's revenue rose by 33% to 1.34 billion yuan in the year from 1.01 billion yuan a year prior.

The higher revenue was mainly due to a steady growth in China's economy, an expansion of service scope into new energy vehicles and household appliances, substantial investment in technological R&D and innovation, and the enhancement of the effectiveness of its AI interactive terminal network.

data.title: Beam Therapeutics Says Initial Data Show BEAM-302 Increases Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Protein
data.description:
Beam Therapeutics said Monday that initial data from a phase 1/2 trial of BEAM-302 show that a single dose led to "durable, dose-dependent" increases in total and functional alpha-1 antitrypsin, or AAT, protein across three initial dose levels, production of corrected protein M-AAT, and reduction in the amount of mutant protein Z-AAT in circulation.

BEAM-302 was well-tolerated at all dose levels, with an "acceptable" safety profile at all dose levels and no serious adverse events observed, the company also said.

AAT deficiency, or AATD, is a genetic disorder affecting the lungs and/or liver, leading to emphysema and liver disease.

Part A of the trial aimed to evaluate AATD in patients with lung disease, while part B will evaluate AATD patients with mild to moderate liver disease with or without lung disease, according to the company.

Beam said it intends to enroll and dose a fourth dose group in part A of the study and dose the first participant in part B in H2.

Shares of the company were down 2.6% in recent Monday premarket activity.
